Description: File Delaware C-Corp via Stripe Atlas, Clerky, or local US counsel. Holding entity for IP, equity, international staff. Pre-condition for any SAFE close. Owner: Teny.
Engage Juba lawyer to register the local operating company. Required for MoH contracts, local bank account, local payroll. Allow 3-4 weeks lead time.

Sign 4-year vesting with 1-year cliff for all 5 (or 6) founders. Templates available via Stripe Atlas / Clerky. Allocation per April playbook: founders 60-70%, ESOP 12-15%, advisor pool 1-2%, investor reserve 15-25%.
Set aside 12-15% Employee Stock Option Pool at incorporation so first hires (and the 6th team member) can receive equity without a renegotiation later.

Summary: [Corp] Operating agreement + IP assignment for all founders
Description: Standard IP assignment: every line of code, design, and clinical content created to date is assigned to the C-Corp. Without this, fundraising due diligence stalls.

Summary: [Ops] Stand up Notion + Slack + role definitions
Description: Notion workspace with: this update doc, role charters, weekly review template, decision log. Slack channels: #general, #eng, #clinical, #ops, #fundraising. Master doc copied in as source of truth.

Summary: [Ops] Open business banking + accounting (Xero or QBO)
Description: Mercury or Brex for the C-Corp. Local bank for SS Ltd. Multi-currency (USD + SSP). Bookkeeper on retainer.

Profile recommendation: senior backend or full-stack engineer to share the hardening load with Valens. Alternative profile: Juba-based implementation lead if Ekow is not local. Onboarded by June 1 ideally.

Summary: [Fundraising] Refresh seed deck with current technical state
Description: 10-12 slides. Lead with: ~104k LOC platform, FHIR R4, offline-first, 8 role dashboards, AI diagnosis, RBAC. Honest about: 20 stubbed APIs, mobile scaffold-only, no CI/CD yet — paired with the May 31 cutover plan. Title slide = company; Page 2 = single-line 'why now' for South Sudan.

Tiers: African health-tech focused (Future Africa, Atlantica, Novastar, AAIC, Catalyst Fund, AHL), impact (Mercy Corps Ventures, IFC TechEmerge, GSMA), generalists with health interest. Track in HubSpot Free or Notion. Warm intro path noted for each.
YC post-money SAFE template. Cap negotiable; valuation cap suggestion $5-8M post-money. Funds runway through pilot launch. Brief existing network week of May 7.
Target: $500k-$1.5M. Begin warm intros to top 10 funds. First meetings target June. Pilot MOU in hand by mid-June is the milestone that closes.
Non-dilutive. Frame around: maternal/child health module + DHIS2 integration + offline reality. Co-write with Chinonye on clinical impact section.
USAID Development Innovation Ventures + Global Innovation Fund. Stage 1 = $200k. Pair with measurable pilot KPIs once anchor site is signed.

Summary: [Fundraising] Recruit 3 advisors (MoH physician, EHR architect, African investor)
Description: 0.25-0.5% each, 24-month vest, no cliff (per FAST template). Personal outreach to 15 candidates targeting 30% conversion. Exec summary one-pager pre-built.
Founder Institute FAST agreement, customized for vesting schedule and confidentiality. Template lives in Notion.

Use ICO (UK) DPIA template adapted for South Sudan. Cover: PHI inventory, lawful basis, retention, sub-processors (AWS, Sentry), cross-border transfer (af-south-1), patient consent flow. Required by any serious donor.

Summary: [Compliance] Draft privacy policy + Terms of Service
Description: Patient-facing privacy policy in English, Arabic, Juba Arabic. Clinician ToS separate. Both reference the DPIA.

One-page architecture note: where PHI lives (AWS af-south-1 / Cape Town), how backups are encrypted, what crosses borders, what would change if MoH demands in-country hosting. Required artifact for MoH and donors.

Summary: [Compliance] Patient consent forms (English, Arabic, Juba Arabic)
Description: Written consent for data collection at clinical onboarding. Plain language. Translated by native speakers, not Google Translate. Add Dinka and Nuer in v2 once anchor site demographics are known.

Summary: [Compliance] Clinical safety case (Class I medical software)
Description: Document classification rationale, hazard log, clinical decision support disclaimers (especially for AI diagnosis), failure modes. EHRs without binding decision support are typically Class I; document the safety case before pilot.
Bowmans, ALN, or East-Africa health-law boutique. Retainer model. First task: review DPIA + privacy policy. Second: position for export to Kenya (DPA 2019), Rwanda, South Africa POPIA.
Written runbook: detection, triage, containment, MoH notification within 24h, patient notification, post-mortem template. Test once before launch.

Summary: [Clinical] Validate all clinical workflows in MVP
Description: Walk every existing workflow (consultation, lab orders, pharmacy, antenatal/WHO 8-contact, EPI, CRVS, surveillance) end-to-end in staging with realistic patient scenarios. Sign off or file specific bugs.

Summary: [Clinical] Sign off on AI diagnosis logic for 15 diseases
Description: Review symptom-to-diagnosis mapping for all 15. Document decision support disclaimers (this is not a diagnostic device; clinician judgment required). Required for safety case.
Identify the next ~50 codes most relevant to South Sudan disease burden (malaria, TB, malnutrition, maternal/perinatal, NCDs). Working list reviewable by MoH if needed.
Apply for IHTSDO low-income license (free for South Sudan). Map ICD-11 codes to SNOMED CT terms. Implementation in platform after May 31 cutover.
2-day curriculum for clinicians (nurses, clinical officers, doctors). Mix didactic + role-play + live system exercises. Co-design with Ekow on logistics.

Summary: [Clinical] DHIS2 export field mapping with MoH
Description: Confirm with MoH which HMIS reports they expect, frequency, format. Map TamamHealth fields to DHIS2 dataset elements. Test export with sample data.

Summary: [Security] Remove hardcoded demo creds from seed data
Description: dr.wani / Dr.Wani@JTH2026 cannot ship. Even behind NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE, an env flip exposes them. Replace with randomly generated creds printed once at seed time.

Summary: [Infra] Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
Description: Workflows for: lint, type-check, run 6 core test suites, build all components, deploy to staging on merge to main, manual approval to prod. Required to stop manual deploys before any pilot.

Summary: [BE] Auto-apply Postgres DB migrations on deploy
Description: Existing 364 LOC schema never auto-applied. Use Prisma Migrate / Knex / node-pg-migrate. Migration pipeline part of CI/CD. Idempotent.

Summary: [BE] CSRF token enforcement on all mutations
Description: SameSite cookies alone are insufficient. Add per-session CSRF token, validate on every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE. Document the threat model.

Summary: [BE] Enforce token blacklist on logout / mutating routes
Description: token-blacklist.ts exists but is not enforced. Wire into auth middleware so revoked JWTs are rejected. Required for clinical-context logout (shared devices).
In-process limiting breaks under horizontal scaling. Redis (Upstash for ease) with sliding-window or token-bucket algorithm. Code comment already flags this.

Summary: [BE] Wire audit_log writes to all mutations
Description: audit_log table exists but no rows are ever written. Add middleware/decorator pattern: every mutation logs (user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, timestamp, source_ip). Immutable. Required for HIPAA-equivalent posture and MoH audit.
Stop putting JWT secret, DB password, API keys in .env files. Doppler or 1Password Secrets Automation. Rotation policy documented (90 days for non-PHI keys, 30 days for PHI-adjacent).
Current backup writes to same host volume — single point of failure. Daily encrypted snapshots to S3 (af-south-1, separate account) or Backblaze B2. Quarterly restore drill documented.
Budget $5-10k. Cobalt, HackerOne, or local boutique. Scope: platform API, auth, CouchDB exposure. Commission Week 2 so report lands by May 28 with 3 days of remediation buffer.

Sentry for error tracking (already in stack). Add Better Stack / UptimeRobot for endpoint uptime. PagerDuty or Slack alerts for: 5xx rate >1%, latency p95 >2s, auth failures >50/min, DB connection failures.
Cape Town region. Cutover from current setup. Caddy auto-TLS, healthchecks, blue-green or canary on platform. Document the rollback path.
Insurance/cash mix typical for SS clinics. Patient-level invoicing, line items map to encounters. FHIR Account/ChargeItem optional v2.
Patient insurance/scheme lookup. Even if just MoH-vs-cash for pilot, design for future expansion.
ESI (Emergency Severity Index) or similar. Tied to encounter creation. CMO sign-off on triage rules.
Inventory by blood type, expiry tracking, issue/return events. Integrate with consultation when transfusion ordered.
Stock levels for essential drugs (WHO EML), reorder points, consumption analytics. Most commonly used after consultations.
Per-patient emergency contacts, allergies, do-not-do orders. Read-heavy, low write.
Shift roster by department. Could defer to June if API completion time-boxed.
Inter-ward and inter-facility transfers. Tied to FHIR Encounter with location chain.
Ward and bed inventory. CRUD + occupancy queries.
Defer to June. Not blocker for single-facility pilot. Daily.co or Twilio Video integration.
Analytics DB has schema but no UPSERT logic. Needed once mobile app starts double-writing offline batches. Document the conflict-resolution rule per table (last-write-wins vs merge).
Replace mock auth screen with real /auth/login + /auth/refresh. Secure storage via Expo SecureStore. Biometric unlock optional v2.
Replace hardcoded profile mock with real GET /patients/me. Edit flow with PATCH and optimistic update.
List, view, request appointment. Push notification (Expo Push) on confirmation.
Read-only patient view of their own encounters, labs, prescriptions. PHI handling care: never cached unencrypted.
Mirror the platform's PouchDB <-> CouchDB pattern on the patient app. Conflict resolution: server-wins for clinical data; client-wins for patient-entered notes.

Summary: [Mobile] Build and deploy beta to TestFlight + Play internal track
Description: Closed beta with pilot site clinicians and ~10 patient testers. EAS Build via Expo. Internal distribution only until June 30.
Ranked. Mix: 1 NGO-run clinic (IRC/MSF/IMC/PIH), 1 mission/faith-based hospital, 1 private clinic in Juba. Each with named contact, decision-maker, and known budget reality.
Standard MOU: scope, duration (6 months), data ownership (TamamHealth retains aggregated/de-identified rights), pricing (free/subsidized for first 5 sites), exit clause, joint case study commitment.

Summary: [Ops] Negotiate and sign anchor pilot MOU
Description: Convert top shortlist site to signed MOU. Co-led with Teny on relationship side. This is the milestone that unlocks pre-seed close.
Plan finalized May 21; order placed June 7 latest (4-week customs window for South Sudan). Tablets (Samsung Galaxy Tab A series, 8-10"), SIMs (MTN South Sudan), data plans, charging infra.
WhatsApp Business account, phone line, ticket triage in Crisp or Intercom. Initial coverage hours, escalation path to Valens for critical.
Mean time to resolution <24h for non-critical, <4h for clinical-impact. Uptime 99% target. Document so MoH can see it later.
Co-built with Chinonye's curriculum. Travel logistics for trainers. On-site presence for first 2 weeks of pilot non-negotiable.
Even an introductory courtesy meeting counts. Position: not asking for approval today, building relationship. MoH engagement is the longest lead time — start week 1.
WHO country office. Frame around DHIS2 integration and HMIS reporting. They can route to MoH if direct route is slow.
Warm intros where possible. Pilot site shortlist already targets at least one NGO-run clinic. NGOs decide locally and have budget.
Goal: by end of June, a letter from MoH acknowledging the TamamHealth pilot. Materially de-risks fundraising even short of formal endorsement.
